mnemonics.title: Fill Rate Formula
mnemonics.content: Fill Rate = Pixels × Colors × Frame Rate. Remember: 'People Consistently Forget Real Numbers' - Pixels, Colors, Frame Rate.

mnemonics.title: GPU Bottleneck Types
mnemonics.content: CPU bottleneck = Low GPU usage, high CPU. GPU bottleneck = High GPU usage. Memory bottleneck = High memory bandwidth, stalling. Remember: 'Vertex, Fragment, Memory - VFM'

mnemonics.title: Performance Triangle
mnemonics.content: Frame Rate vs Resolution vs Visual Quality - Pick two, sacrifice the third. Similar to 'Fast, Cheap, Good - Pick Two'.

mnemonics.title: Pipeline Stages Memory
mnemonics.content: Application → Vertex Shader → Rasterization → Fragment Shader → Output Merger. Remember: 'All Vast Rendered Fragments Outstanding'.

acronyms.usage: Primary measure of rendering speed. 60 FPS = 16.67ms per frame budget.
Triangle throughput - how many triangles GPU can process per second.
Fill rate measurement - pixels rendered per second.
Memory bandwidth - data transfer rate to/from video memory.
Dedicated graphics memory for textures, framebuffers, and geometry data.
Software interface like DirectX, OpenGL, Vulkan for GPU communication.
Technique to reduce jagged edges, significantly impacts fill rate (4× for 4xMSAA).
Final stage that writes pixel data to framebuffer, affects fill rate.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a bucket filling pixels. Resolution = bucket size, fill rate = tap speed, fps = how often you need a full bucket. Larger bucket or faster filling needed for higher resolution/fps.
Think of memory bandwidth as highway lanes. Textures = trucks (wide, slow), vertices = motorcycles (small, fast). More lanes (256-bit vs 128-bit) = more traffic capacity.
Graphics pipeline = assembly line. Vertex shader = design team, Rasterization = cutting team, Fragment shader = painting team. Bottleneck = slowest team holding up entire factory.

Texture cache = small pantry next to stove. Frequently used items (spices) = cached textures. Running to main storage (supermarket) = slow main memory access. Keep 'cooking ingredients' (textures) in pantry.

analogies.analogy: Frame rate is like frequency of bus departures (60 buses/hour). Latency is how long it takes for a single passenger to reach destination. High frame rate doesn't guarantee low latency if buses take wrong routes.
GPU bottleneck = engine limited (car can't go faster). CPU bottleneck = driver limited (car waiting for driver commands). Check who's limiting before upgrading.
Resolution = screen real estate size. Doubling resolution = 4× more pixels to paint. Like going from painting small posters to billboard-sized ads with same time.
Compressed textures = zipped files. Takes less space (storage/bandwidth) but needs unzipping (GPU decode time). Trade-off between storage and processing.
